University of San Francisco v. Brian Mendel, et al. CGC -09-489122
FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION—Breach of Contract
(number)
ATTACHMENT TO [_] Complaint Cross - Complaint
(Use a separate cause of action form for each cause of action.)
BC-1. Plaintiff (name): University of San Francisco ("the University")
alleges that on or about (date): June 12, 2004
a LX] written LX] oral LX] other (specify): implied by conduct
agreement was made between (name parties to agreement): The University and Cross-Defendant Brian
Mendel
A copy of the agreement is attached as Exhibit A, or
The essential terms of the agreement [__] are stated in Attachment BC-1 [X_] are as follows (specify):
During the period July 12, 2004 to October 9, 2008, Cross-Defendant was an employee
employed by the University in its Office of Business and Finance, as an Assistant Controller.
As an employee of the University, Cross-Defendant owed duties and obligations to his
employer, the University, as provided by law and by the contractual terms and conditions of
his employment, to not convert University property and to not conceal his possession of
University property, either during the employment or thereafter.
BC-2. On or about (dates): 10/8/2008 - present
defendant breached the agreement by the acts specified in Attachment BC-2. [__] the following acts
(specify):
BC-3. Plaintiff has performed all obligations to defendant except those obligations plaintiff was prevented or
excused from performing.
BC-4. Plaintiff suffered damages legally (proximately) caused by defendant's breach of the agreement
[_] as stated in Attachment BC-4 as follows (specify):
Cross-Defendant’s acts and conducts as described in Attachment BC-2, interfered with
and continues to interfere with the University’s right to sole and exclusive use, possession
and ownership of its property and/or interference with the University’s efforts to protect
the privacy of the University’s financial and proprietary information, causing damage in
an amount in excess of $25,000, to be proven at trial.
BC-5. [__] Plaintiff is entitled to attorney fees by an agreement or a statute
[_] of $
[__] according to proof.
BC-6. [X] Other:
Cross-Complainant will seek leave to amend as Cross-Defendants, those other
individuals, attorneys, agents and representatives of Cross-Defendant, who are in receipt,
possession or control of the University property, to be discovered.

1 Attachment No. BC-2

2 I. The University’s Business and Finance Office handles confidential and/or

3 || proprietary financial information and documents, including statements of cash flow, assets and

4 || liabilities, investments, receivables, expenses and property (collectively herein, “Property of the

5 || University”).

6 2. Cross-Defendant agreed that as an employee of the University, he would not

7 ||remove to his sole possession the Property of the University, without its consent or that he

8 || would not destroy or convert Property of the University.

9 3. Beginning on or about October 8, 2008 and continuing thereafter to dates that are
10 || currently unknown, Cross-Defendant secretly, surreptiously, and without authorization from the
11 || University, removed Property of the University to his sole and exclusive possession.

12 4. In an effort to prevent the University from discovering his actions as described in
13. || paragraph 3 above, and/or to prevent the University from having use of the Property of the
14 || University, Cross-Defendant deleted from University computer systems and network, certain
15 {|documents and then removed to his possession, to his home, and to other locations to be
16 || discovered, such University property.
17 5. Cross-Defendant has thereafter at all times, failed and refused to return the
18 || University’s property and to provide an accounting to the University of all locations, disclosures
19 || and copies of the University’s property.
20 6. Beginning on or about October 8, 2008 and continuing thereafter to dates that are
21 || currently unknown, Cross-Defendant secretly, surreptiously, and without authorization from the
22 || University, destroyed University property by deleting electronic documents from University
23 ||computers; and has thereafter at all times, failed and refused to provide an accounting to the
24 || University of the nature and extent of these acts.
25 7. As described in paragraphs 1 through 6 above, Cross-Defendant (a) intentionally
26 ||converted Property of the University; (b) intentionally destroyed University property; and (c)
27 |{engaged in other intentional acts, in violation of his duties under Cal. Labor Code §§ 2850-
28 || 2854; 2856; 2860-2865.

USE Human Resources
UNIVERSITY of zge Fulton Street
SAN FRANCISCO San Francisco, CA 94117-1080
TEL 415 422-6707

July 7, 2004

Brian Mendel

2636 Birchtree Lane

Santa Clara, CA 95051

Dear Mr. Mendel:

Congratulations on your appointment as Senior Accountant ~ Fixed Asset in the
Office of Business & Finance effective July 12, 2004. Your annual salary will be
$68,000/yr. You are also eligible for University provided benefits, subject to the terms of
policies and benefits plans, which may change from time to time.

To assist you in the employment process, please visit the Human Resources Office
(Campion Hall, Room C-7) on your first day of work. At that time, you will complete the
necessary personnel/payroll employment forms.

Please contact Victoria Hermoso, our Benefits Specialist, your first day of work at .
(415) 422-2834 to schedule an appointment for your benefits orientation and enrollment.
Because your start date is July 12, 2004, the earliest your medical coverage will begin
is August 1, 2004. Delay in obtaining your benefits orientation will delay the date of
your medical coverage.

Aiso, we invite you to our next availabie New Staff Orientation on Tuesday, August 3,
2004 at 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m., in the Maraschi Room, Xavier Hall. You may call
Human Resources at (415) 422-6707 to confirm your attendance. We look forward to
your participation.

The University of San Francisco, in compliance with the Immigration Reform and
Control Act of 1986, requires that all new employees must provide evidence of :
employment eligibility. To comply with these regulations, you must present original
documents which attest to your identity and right to work in the United States. We will
make copies for our records and return the originals to you immediately. The attached
sheet lists documents sufficient to complete the 1-9 process. Upon successful
completion of the I-9 process, we will issue you a USF ID card form.

In accordance with the Administrative Staff Handbook, al! newly hired administrators,
such as you, will serve a twelve-month evaluation period, beginning with the first day of
employment. This period is designed to allow an appropriate orientation and evaluation
period for the administrator. It also provides time for administrators to assess their
aptitude and interest in the job. During the evaluation period the employment
relationship is “at will.” The administrator is free to resign at will at any time, with or
without cause. Similarly, the University may terminate the employment relationship at
will at any time, with or without cause. The evaluation period may be extended by the
supervisor for a maximum of three additionat months as written notice to you. For

the Associate Vice President, Human Resources.
Please confirm your acceptance of these terms of the University's offer of employment
and acknowledgement of receipt of the Handbook by signing and returning the original
copy of this letter'to the Human Resources Office at your earliest convenience. Should
you have any questions, please call Human Resources at (415) 422-6707.
We welcome you to the University of San Francisco and wish you well in your new
position.
